questions.text: An ASN is always required to create an Inbound Delivery in NEO.
What are two key differences between a "Rich ASN" and a "Poor ASN"?
options: "Rich ASN" includes an SSCC (Serial Shipping Container Code), while a "Poor ASN" does not. This impacts how the system groups items into Inbound Deliveries.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the "Delivery Grouping Rule" in NEO?
options: It determines how Inbound Deliveries are created from the ASN, depending on factors like material, order, batch, and SSCC. This rule allows flexibility based on the WMS capabilities.

questions.text: Name one key report that can be used to monitor stock levels in NEO.
options: Stock in Transit report (GCP) or Stock report - Multiple materials – NEO (Both are valid)

questions.text: What triggers the automatic generation of an Inbound Delivery in the BOM Header Reception process?
options: Manual entry by the MSC procurement planner
The ASN received from the co-packer via interface
Completion of the MRP run by the co-packer

questions.text: The objective of the GS1 Rules for SSCC is to always transform the SSCC into an 18-digit format, regardless of whether the plant is maintained in the TVARVC table.
